title: Blackmar-Diemer Gambit
shortDescription: A tactical gambit starting 1.d4 d5 2.e4, sacrificing a pawn for rapid development and open lines.

root.comment: White starts with the queen's pawn.

children.comment: The Blackmar-Diemer Gambit offer: White invites ...dxe4.
White develops and attacks the e4 pawn.
The key gambit move: White offers a second pawn to rip open the center and f-file.
White recaptures, ready to castle and develop quickly. Two extra tempi for the pawn.
The Bogoljubov Defense: Black pins the knight immediately.
White asks the bishop a question before it can be reinforced.
White has the bishop pair, a lead in development, and open lines for one pawn.

line.name: Accepted: Bogoljubov Defense (5...Bg4)
line.description: The main tabiya of the BDG. Black grabs the pawn and develops with ...Bg4, pinning the knight.

children.comment: Black supports the center and prepares a solid setup.
Classical development: White prepares queenside castling.
White commits to opposite-side attacking chances.

line.name: Accepted: Teichmann Defense - 6...Bxf3 Classical
line.description: Main accepted BDG line with 5...Bg4, where Black pins the knight and exchanges on f3. White usually gets active development and open attacking chances.

children.comment: Aggressive: White is ready to give up d4 for attacking tempi.
White drives the knight and keeps attacking.

line.name: Accepted: Teichmann Defense - Seidel-Hall Attack
line.description: A sharper Teichmann line where White sacrifices more material with g4 to gain kingside initiative.

children.comment: White keeps d4 protected and prepares development.

line.name: Accepted: Teichmann Defense - Qf2 / Ciesielski Setup
line.description: A quieter setup where White defends d4 and prepares long-term development rather than immediate g-pawn aggression.

children.comment: White kicks the bishop and starts kingside expansion.
Centralizes the knight and increases pressure.

line.name: Accepted: Teichmann Defense - 6...Bh5 Retreat
line.description: Instead of exchanging on f3, Black retreats the bishop and keeps tension. White gains space with g4 and often jumps with Ne5.

children.comment: The solid Teichmann Defense: Black declines further complications and prepares ...Be7.
White pins the f6 knight and prepares long castling for an attack.

line.name: Accepted: Teichmann Defense (5...e6)
line.description: Black returns the pawn setup and opts for a solid, French-like structure instead of grabbing more material.

children.comment: Black immediately attacks White's center.

line.name: Accepted: Euwe Defense - 7.Bd3 c5
line.description: Euwe Defense with 5...e6. Black reaches a French-like extra-pawn structure and challenges d4 with ...c5.

children.comment: White avoids immediate recapture and keeps attacking possibilities.

line.name: Accepted: Euwe Defense - Zilbermints Gambit
line.description: A risky attacking continuation where White allows ...Nxd4 and plays for initiative rather than material.

children.comment: White uses the rook actively after long castling.

line.name: Accepted: Euwe Defense - Qd2 and Long Castling
line.description: White chooses a queenside castling plan and tries to generate kingside attacking chances.

line.name: Accepted: Euwe Defense - ...h6 and Bh4
line.description: Black questions the bishop before completing development. White keeps attacking chances but must be precise.

children.comment: Bogoljubov Defense: Black fianchettoes and pressures d4.
Studier Attack setup.
White prepares Qh4 and kingside pressure.

line.name: Accepted: Bogoljubov Defense - Studier Attack
line.description: Bogoljubov Defense with 5...g6. White uses Bc4, castling, Qe1-h4 and kingside attacking ideas.

children.comment: A flexible development square before Qd2 and long castling.
Black strikes at White's center.
White gains space and creates tactical problems.

line.name: Accepted: Bogoljubov Defense - Queenside Castling Setup
line.description: White develops with Bf4 and Qd2, aiming to castle long and attack with h-pawn ideas.

line.name: Accepted: Bogoljubov Defense - 6.Bd3 Solid Black Setup
line.description: White directly develops the bishop, but Black can aim for a compact setup with ...c6, ...e6 and ...Be7.

children.comment: Black develops actively before playing ...e6.

line.name: Accepted: Gunderam / Tartakower Defense - 5...Bf5 6.Ne5
line.description: Black develops the bishop actively to f5. White jumps into e5 and starts kingside expansion.

line.name: Accepted: Gunderam / Tartakower Defense - Bc4 Setup
line.description: White develops calmly with Bc4 and castles, keeping pressure while avoiding immediate pawn storms.

children.comment: Ziegler Defense: Black builds a compact center.

line.name: Accepted: Ziegler Defense - 5...c6 6.Bd3
line.description: Ziegler Defense. Black plays ...c6, aiming for solidity and control over d5/e4 structures.

line.name: Accepted: Ziegler Defense - 6.Bc4
line.description: White chooses direct piece development and kingside pressure against Black's solid ...c6 setup.

line.name: Accepted: Pietrowsky Defense - 5...Nc6
line.description: Black develops the queenside knight quickly. White usually develops naturally and keeps attacking pressure.

children.comment: The Ryder Gambit: White recaptures with the queen, eyeing Qxf6 and gxf7 ideas.
Black shores up d5 and prepares ...Qc7 or ...Qb6.
White continues developing toward the kingside for a quick attack.

line.name: Ryder Gambit (5.Qxf3)
line.description: A sharper try: instead of recapturing with the knight, White recaptures with the queen to threaten Qxf6 and pile onto the f-file immediately.

children.comment: White accepts the material deficit and plays for activity.

line.name: Ryder Gambit - 5.Qxf3 c6
line.description: The Ryder Gambit: White recaptures with the queen instead of the knight, playing for immediate threats but accepting risk.

line.name: Ryder Gambit - Early ...Qxd4 and ...Qg4
line.description: A common defensive idea: Black grabs d4 and tries to simplify or harass the queen.

line.name: Ryder Gambit - 5...e6 6.Bc4 Nc6
line.description: Black plays solidly and develops, challenging White to justify the queen recapture.

children.comment: Black delays ...exf3 and develops actively.

line.name: BDG: 4...Bf5 Vienna-Type Defense - 5.fxe4
line.description: Black develops the bishop before accepting on f3. White can simply recapture on e4.

children.comment: White immediately challenges the bishop.
White finally restores the pawn on e4.

line.name: BDG: 4...Bf5 Kampars-Style g-pawn Attack
line.description: White throws the g-pawn forward before recapturing, gaining space and forcing the bishop back.

children.comment: White continues the pawn storm.

line.name: BDG: 4...Bf5 Gunderam Attack
line.description: A direct kingside expansion with g4 and h4 against the developed bishop.

children.comment: O'Kelly Defense.

line.name: BDG: O'Kelly Defense - 4...c6
line.description: Black plays ...c6 before accepting or resolving the center, aiming for a compact defensive structure.

children.comment: Brombacher Countergambit.

line.name: BDG: Brombacher Countergambit - 4...c5
line.description: Black immediately counterattacks the center with ...c5 instead of accepting on f3.

children.comment: Langeheinecke Defense: Black pushes the extra pawn forward.

line.name: BDG: Langeheinecke Defense - 4...e3
line.description: Black advances the e-pawn to e3, forcing White to spend time resolving it.

children.comment: Elbert Countergambit.

line.name: BDG: Elbert Countergambit - 4...e5
line.description: Black immediately counterstrikes with ...e5 after 4.f3, creating a tactical central fight.

children.comment: Weinsbach Declined.

line.name: BDG: Weinsbach Declined - 4...e6
line.description: Black declines the immediate capture on f3 and goes for solid development.

children.comment: Studier-Rasa idea: developing before f3.

line.name: BDG Sideline: Studier-Rasa Gambit - 4.Be3
line.description: Instead of immediate f3, White develops the bishop first and keeps flexible gambit ideas.

children.comment: Von Popiel Gambit setup.

line.name: BDG Sideline: Von Popiel Gambit - 4.Bg5
line.description: White pins the knight before playing f3, aiming for quick development and pressure.

children.comment: Lemberger Countergambit.
White takes on e4 and accepts a different type of game.

line.name: Lemberger Countergambit - 4.Nxe4
line.description: After 3...e5 Black counterattacks the center instead of defending e4. 4.Nxe4 is one practical response.

line.name: Lemberger Countergambit - 4.dxe5 Queen Trade
line.description: A calmer approach against the Lemberger, often leading to early queen exchange and less gambit-style play.

line.name: Lemberger Countergambit - 4.Qh5
line.description: A more provocative anti-Lemberger try, keeping queens on and looking for attacking chances.

children.comment: Black supports e4 with a Netherlands-style pawn move.

line.name: Declined / Sideline: 3...f5 Netherlands-Type Defense
line.description: Black supports the e4 pawn with ...f5 and creates a Dutch-like structure.

children.comment: Black declines the pawn, transposing toward French Defense structures.
White pins the knight and keeps developing naturally, still with central space.

line.name: Declined: 2...e6 (French Structure)
line.description: Black declines the gambit and heads toward a French-like structure, avoiding early tactics.

line.name: Declined: 2...e6 French Structure
line.description: Black declines the gambit immediately and heads into French Defense structures.

children.comment: Black declines with a Caro-Kann style pawn structure.
White develops naturally and keeps a comfortable space advantage.

line.name: Declined: 2...c6 (Caro-Kann Structure)
line.description: Black supports d5 with the c-pawn, aiming for a Caro-Kann style setup instead of taking on e4.

line.name: Declined: 2...c6 Caro-Kann Structure
line.description: Black supports d5 with ...c6 and aims for a Caro-Kann type position.

children.comment: Black counterattacks the e4 pawn rather than capturing it.
White simply takes the attacked pawn.
White develops, attacking the d5 knight and preparing e4 again.

line.name: Declined: 2...Nf6
line.description: Black attacks e4 with a knight instead of capturing it, but the undefended d5 pawn simply falls.

line.name: Declined: 2...Nf6 Counterattack
line.description: Black attacks e4 rather than accepting the gambit. White can often take on d5 and develop with tempo.
